title: Ranunculus cauda-muris
intro:
There is no information on the plant "Ranunculus cauda-muris". However, I found information on the plant Ranunculus caudatus, which is sometimes referred to as Ranunculus cauda-muris in some classifications, and it is a species of flowering plant.

Ranunculus caudatus is a plant species that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.

The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus caudatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making the plant a potential natural remedy for various health issues.

Ranunculus caudatus is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures. The most common preparations made with this plant include a decoction or infusion of the dried roots and rhizomes, which can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
